what: What a raw Gapminder sheet looks like
title: Getting started with tidygapminder
text: typical Gapminder sheet looks like tidying: first column header holds indicator name (life expectancy years), every subsequent column year. wide format makes hard filter year, plot trends, join indicators.
code: csv_path <- system.file("extdata/life_expectancy_years.csv",                          package = "tidygapminder")  raw <- read.csv(csv_path, check.names = FALSE)  # Indicator name is in the first column header colnames(raw)[1:6] #> [1] "country" "1800"    "1801"    "1802"    "1803"    "1804"  # Countries are rows, years are columns head(raw[, 1:6]) #>               country 1800 1801 1802 1803 1804 #> 1         Afghanistan 28.2 28.2 28.2 28.2 28.2 #> 2             Albania 35.4 35.4 35.4 35.4 35.4 #> 3             Algeria 28.8 28.8 28.8 28.8 28.8 #> 4             Andorra   NA   NA   NA   NA   NA #> 5              Angola 27.0 27.0 27.0 27.0 27.0 #> 6 Antigua and Barbuda 33.5 33.5 33.5 33.5 33.5

what: Tidying a single sheet with tidy_index()
title: Getting started with tidygapminder
text: tidy_index() takes path single Gapminder sheet (.csv, .xlsx, .xls) returns tidy tibble three columns: country, year, indicator. row now one observation. indicator column named file, matches Gapminder convention naming files indicator. tidy_index() also handles .xlsx files identically:
code: tidy_df <- tidy_index(csv_path)  head(tidy_df) #> # A tibble: 6 × 3 #>   country              year life_expectancy_years #>   <chr>               <dbl>                 <dbl> #> 1 Afghanistan          1800                  28.2 #> 2 Albania              1800                  35.4 #> 3 Algeria              1800                  28.8 #> 4 Andorra              1800                  NA   #> 5 Angola               1800                  27   #> 6 Antigua and Barbuda  1800                  33.5 xlsx_path <- system.file("extdata/agriculture_land.xlsx",                           package = "tidygapminder")  tidy_index(xlsx_path) #> # A tibble: 11,076 × 3 #>    country              year `Agricultural land (% of land area)` #>    <chr>               <dbl>                                <dbl> #>  1 Afghanistan          1960                                   NA #>  2 Albania              1960                                   NA #>  3 Algeria              1960                                   NA #>  4 American Samoa       1960                                   NA #>  5 Andorra              1960                                   NA #>  6 Angola               1960                                   NA #>  7 Antigua and Barbuda  1960                                   NA #>  8 Argentina            1960                                   NA #>  9 Armenia              1960                                   NA #> 10 Aruba                1960                                   NA #> # ℹ 11,066 more rows

what: Tidying a folder of sheets with tidy_bunch()
title: Getting started with tidygapminder
text: working multiple indicators , tidy_bunch() applies tidy_index() every compatible file directory returns named list tibbles — one per file:
code: dir_path <- system.file("extdata", package = "tidygapminder")  result <- tidy_bunch(dir_path)  # One tibble per file, named after the indicator names(result) #> [1] "agriculture_land"      "life_expectancy_years"  head(result$life_expectancy_years) #> # A tibble: 6 × 3 #>   country              year life_expectancy_years #>   <chr>               <dbl>                 <dbl> #> 1 Afghanistan          1800                  28.2 #> 2 Albania              1800                  35.4 #> 3 Algeria              1800                  28.8 #> 4 Andorra              1800                  NA   #> 5 Angola               1800                  27   #> 6 Antigua and Barbuda  1800                  33.5

previous_headings: Tidying a folder of sheets with tidy_bunch()
what: Combining all indicators into one data frame
title: Getting started with tidygapminder
text: Setting combine = TRUE merges tibbles single data frame joined country year, using full outer join observations lost even indicators cover different time ranges: combined format convenient multi-indicator analyses, example plotting life expectancy agricultural land use per country.
code: combined <- tidy_bunch(dir_path, combine = TRUE)  head(combined) #> # A tibble: 6 × 4 #>   country      year `Agricultural land (% of land area)` life_expectancy_years #>   <chr>       <dbl>                                <dbl>                 <dbl> #> 1 Afghanistan  1800                                   NA                  28.2 #> 2 Afghanistan  1801                                   NA                  28.2 #> 3 Afghanistan  1802                                   NA                  28.2 #> 4 Afghanistan  1803                                   NA                  28.2 #> 5 Afghanistan  1804                                   NA                  28.2 #> 6 Afghanistan  1805                                   NA                  28.2

title: Getting started with tidygapminder
text: functions provide informative errors common mistakes:
code: # File does not exist tidy_index("path/to/missing_file.csv") #> Error in `tidy_index()`: #> ! Input file not found: path/to/missing_file.csv  # Unsupported format tidy_index(tempfile(fileext = ".ods")) #> Error in `tidy_index()`: #> ! Input file not found: /tmp/RtmpZqoDhC/file1a0c348e0df2.ods  # Directory does not exist tidy_bunch("path/to/missing_dir") #> Error in `tidy_bunch()`: #> ! Directory not found: path/to/missing_dir

title: Easily Tidy Gapminder Datasets
text: Gapminder data, minus mess. Gapminder goldmine global development data — life expectancy, income, CO₂ emissions, literacy rates, hundreds indicators spanning centuries. catch? Every sheet looks like : Countries rows, years columns, indicator name hiding cell A1. Great spreadsheet. Terrible R. tidygapminder fixes one function call.

title: Easily Tidy Gapminder Datasets
text: ```{r} # CRAN install.packages(“tidygapminder”)

title: Easily Tidy Gapminder Datasets
text: pak::pak(“ebedthan/tidygapminder”) Three columns: country, year, indicator, ready filter, plot, model.
code: ## Two functions. That's it.  ### `tidy_index()`: one file at a time  Point it at a Gapminder `.csv`, `.xlsx`, or `.xls` file and get back a clean tibble:  ```{r} library(tidygapminder)  csv_path <- system.file("extdata/life_expectancy_years.csv", package = "tidygapminder")  tidy_index(csv_path)

what: tidy_bunch(): a whole folder at once
title: Easily Tidy Gapminder Datasets
text: Downloaded ten indicators? problem. Point tidy_bunch() folder: ```{r} dir_path <- system.file(“extdata”, package = “tidygapminder”)

what: Returns a named list of tibbles, one per file
title: Easily Tidy Gapminder Datasets
text: result <- tidy_bunch(dir_path) names(result)
code: Want everything in one data frame joined by `country` and `year`?  ```{r} tidy_bunch(dir_path, combine = TRUE)

title: Easily Tidy Gapminder Datasets
text: Zero friction: arguments learn beyond file path Handles quirks: indicator name cell A1, non-numeric year columns, mixed file formats: taken care Lightweight: two dependencies (readxl tibble) Informative errors: tells exactly went wrong

title: Easily Tidy Gapminder Datasets
text: Read vignette: vignette("tidygapminder") Browse documentation: https://ebedthan.github.io/tidygapminder/ Found bug? Open issue

what: Tidy Gapminder indicator data sheets stored in a folder. — tidy_bunch
title: Tidy Gapminder indicator data sheets stored in a folder. — tidy_bunch
text: Applies tidy_index Gapminder data sheets found directory optionally combines single data frame.

title: Tidy Gapminder indicator data sheets stored in a folder. — tidy_bunch
Tidy Gapminder indicator data sheets stored in a folder. — tidy_bunch
text: dirpath Path folder containing indices Gapminder indicator data sheets. Defaults current working directory. combine boolean. TRUE, combines tidied data sheets single data frame joined country year. Default:  FALSE. ... Additional arguments (reserved future use).

title: Tidy Gapminder indicator data sheets stored in a folder. — tidy_bunch
text: combine = FALSE (default), named list tibbles, one per file.   combine = TRUE, single tibble indicators merged   country year.

title: Tidy Gapminder indicator data sheets stored in a folder. — tidy_bunch
code: folder_path <- system.file("extdata", package = "tidygapminder")  tidy_bunch(folder_path) #> $agriculture_land #> # A tibble: 11,076 × 3 #>    country              year `Agricultural land (% of land area)` #>    <chr>               <dbl>                                <dbl> #>  1 Afghanistan          1960                                   NA #>  2 Albania              1960                                   NA #>  3 Algeria              1960                                   NA #>  4 American Samoa       1960                                   NA #>  5 Andorra              1960                                   NA #>  6 Angola               1960                                   NA #>  7 Antigua and Barbuda  1960                                   NA #>  8 Argentina            1960                                   NA #>  9 Armenia              1960                                   NA #> 10 Aruba                1960                                   NA #> # ℹ 11,066 more rows #>  #> $life_expectancy_years #> # A tibble: 40,953 × 3 #>    country              year life_expectancy_years #>    <chr>               <dbl>                 <dbl> #>  1 Afghanistan          1800                  28.2 #>  2 Albania              1800                  35.4 #>  3 Algeria              1800                  28.8 #>  4 Andorra              1800                  NA   #>  5 Angola               1800                  27   #>  6 Antigua and Barbuda  1800                  33.5 #>  7 Argentina            1800                  33.2 #>  8 Armenia              1800                  34   #>  9 Australia            1800                  34   #> 10 Austria              1800                  34.4 #> # ℹ 40,943 more rows #>  tidy_bunch(folder_path, combine = TRUE) #> # A tibble: 42,929 × 4 #>    country      year `Agricultural land (% of land area)` life_expectancy_years #>    <chr>       <dbl>                                <dbl>                 <dbl> #>  1 Afghanistan  1800                                   NA                  28.2 #>  2 Afghanistan  1801                                   NA                  28.2 #>  3 Afghanistan  1802                                   NA                  28.2 #>  4 Afghanistan  1803                                   NA                  28.2 #>  5 Afghanistan  1804                                   NA                  28.2 #>  6 Afghanistan  1805                                   NA                  28.2 #>  7 Afghanistan  1806                                   NA                  28.1 #>  8 Afghanistan  1807                                   NA                  28.1 #>  9 Afghanistan  1808                                   NA                  28.1 #> 10 Afghanistan  1809                                   NA                  28.1 #> # code: csv_filepath <- system.file("extdata/life_expectancy_years.csv",                              package = "tidygapminder") xlsx_filepath <- system.file(                  "extdata/agriculture_land.xlsx",                  package = "tidygapminder")  tidy_index(csv_filepath) #> # A tibble: 40,953 × 3 #>    country              year life_expectancy_years #>    <chr>               <dbl>                 <dbl> #>  1 Afghanistan          1800                  28.2 #>  2 Albania              1800                  35.4 #>  3 Algeria              1800                  28.8 #>  4 Andorra              1800                  NA   #>  5 Angola               1800                  27   #>  6 Antigua and Barbuda  1800                  33.5 #>  7 Argentina            1800                  33.2 #>  8 Armenia              1800                  34   #>  9 Australia            1800                  34   #> 10 Austria              1800                  34.4 #> # ℹ 40,943 more rows tidy_index(xlsx_filepath) #> # A tibble: 11,076 × 3 #>    country              year `Agricultural land (% of land area)` #>    <chr>               <dbl>                                <dbl> #>  1 Afghanistan          1960                                   NA #>  2 Albania              1960                                   NA #>  3 Algeria              1960                                   NA #>  4 American Samoa       1960                                   NA #>  5 Andorra              1960                                   NA #>  6 Angola               1960                                   NA #>  7 Antigua and Barbuda  1960                                   NA #>  8 Argentina            1960                                   NA #>  9 Armenia              1960                                   NA #> 10 Aruba                1960                                   NA #> # ℹ 11,066 more rows

title: tidygapminder 1.0.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-05-16

title: tidygapminder 1.0.0
text: tidy_bunch(): merge parameter renamed combine avoid shadowing base::merge(). Update existing calls accordingly. tidy_indice() deprecated favour correctly spelled tidy_index(). still works emit deprecation warning. removed future release.

title: tidygapminder 1.0.0
text: tidy_index() tidy_bunch() now return tibbles instead plain data frames. tidy_bunch() now returns named list (named indicator file) combine = FALSE. tidy_bunch() now supports .xls files addition .csv .xlsx. functions now emit informative error messages missing files, missing directories, unsupported file extensions, empty directories. Year coercion numeric now emits warning values converted.

title: tidygapminder 1.0.0
text: Migrate CI Travis/AppVeyor GitHub Actions. Drop support R < 4.1.0. Upgrade testthat 3rd edition expanded test coverage. Replace readr::read_csv() base read.csv() tidyr::pivot_longer() base reshape(), removing readr tidyr dependencies. Package now depends readxl tibble. pkgdown site upgraded Bootstrap 5.

title: tidygapminder 0.1.1
text: CRAN release: 2020-02-04 Fix bug merging list data frames tidy_bunch().

title: tidygapminder 0.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2019-12-06 First release, tidy_indice() tidy_bunch() functions.
